Name: Threshold Touched
Rules Text: You can interfere with planar movement near you.
Rules Bullets: Prevent Teleport:: When a creature within 10 feet attempts to teleport you may use your reaction to negate the effect.; Threshold Sense:: You have advantage on Wisdom checks to detect planar effects.
Limitations: Reaction based teleport denial tuned as half feat

Name: Veil-Breath Adept
Rules Text: You can survive and function within the Veil without protective equipment.
Rules Bullets: Veil Adaptation:: You can breathe and survive in the Veil without the need for a spacesuit or sealed environment.; Environmental Immunity:: You are immune to suffocation and atmospheric pressure effects caused by the Veil.
Proficiencies Granted: Cannot negate direct damage from Veil entities

Name: Polyglot of the Trade
Rules Text: You have learned the common transactional tongues of the world.
Rules Bullets: Trade Pidgin Fluency:: You can speak read and write Trade Pidgin.; Marillic Fluency:: You can speak read and write Marillic.

Name: Strength Manifest
Rules Text: Your physical force expresses itself with certainty.
Rules Bullets: Forceful Presence:: You count as one size larger when determining carrying capacity and pushing dragging or lifting.; Braced Might:: You have advantage on Strength checks made to resist being moved.

Name: Dexterity Manifest
Rules Text: Your movements resolve with unnatural precision.
Rules Bullets: Reactive Footing:: You gain a +1 bonus to initiative rolls.; Controlled Motion:: You ignore difficult terrain during the first 10 feet of movement each turn.
Modifiers Granted: Does not stack with similar terrain-ignoring features

Name: Constitution Manifest
Rules Text: Your endurance stabilizes your form.
Rules Bullets: Hardened Frame:: When you roll a Hit Die you regain additional hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.; Steady Pulse:: You have advantage on Constitution saves against exhaustion.
Modifiers Granted: Does not grant immunity to exhaustion

Name: Intelligence Manifest
Rules Text: Your cognition accelerates and stabilizes.
Rules Bullets: Analytical Recall:: You gain proficiency in one Intelligence-based skill of your choice.; Logical Defense:: You gain advantage on one Intelligence saving throw per long rest.

Name: Wisdom Manifest
Rules Text: Your perception aligns with reality.
Rules Bullets: Heightened Awareness:: You gain proficiency in Perception or Insight.; Grounded Mind:: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened once per long rest.

Name: Charisma Manifest
Rules Text: Your presence asserts itself without effort.
Rules Bullets: Commanding Tone:: You gain proficiency in Persuasion or Intimidation.; Social Anchor:: You can reroll one failed Charisma check per long rest.

Name: Aeronaut Pilot
Rules Text: You are trained to command aerial vessels.
Rules Bullets: Aerial Vehicles:: You gain proficiency with airships and zeppelins.; Emergency Handling:: When piloting an airship you have advantage on checks to avoid collisions crashes or loss of control.

Name: Abacus Savant
Rules Text: You have mastered the disciplined use of the Abacus.
Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Abacus.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Abacus.

Name: Alchemical Practitioner
Rules Text: You have mastered the disciplined use of the Alchemist Supplies.
Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Alchemist Supplies.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Alchemist Supplies.

Name: Master Brewer
Rules Text: You have mastered the disciplined use of the Brewers Supplies.
Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Brewers Supplies.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Brewers Supplies.

Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Forgery Kit.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Forgery Kit.

Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Herbalism Kit.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Herbalism Kit.

Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Leatherworkers Tools.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Leatherworkers Tools.

Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Masons Tools.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Masons Tools.

Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Navigators Tools.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Navigators Tools.

Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Painters Supplies.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Painters Supplies.

Rules Text: You have mastered the disciplined use of the Poisoners Kit.
Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Poisoners Kit.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Poisoners Kit.

Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Potters Tools.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Potters Tools.

Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Smiths Tools.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Smiths Tools.

Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Tinkers Tools.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Tinkers Tools.

Name: Weaver Artisan
Rules Text: You have mastered the disciplined use of the Weavers Tools.
Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Weavers Tools.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Weavers Tools.

Name: Woodcarver Artisan
Rules Text: You have mastered the disciplined use of the Woodcarvers Tools.
Rules Bullets: Tool Mastery:: You gain proficiency with Woodcarvers Tools.; Expert Handling:: Once per long rest you have advantage on one ability check using Woodcarvers Tools.

Name: Veil-Habituated
Rules Text: You have adapted to prolonged exposure to the Veil.
Rules Bullets: Veil Mitigation:: Environmental hazards from the Veil are treated as one step less severe.; Rest Assimilation:: Short rests taken in the Veil restore one additional Hit Die.; Veil Sense:: You sense thinning or breaches in the Veil within 30 feet.

Name: Iterative Reasoning
Rules Text: You solve problems through refinement and reassessment.
Rules Bullets: Refined Attempt:: After failing an Intelligence check you may retry after 1 minute using a different approach.; False Assumption:: On a success the DM reveals one misleading factor avoided.

Name: Productive Error
Rules Text: Failure produces insight rather than loss.
Rules Bullets: Degraded Success:: When you fail a tool check by 5 or less you achieve a temporary or degraded result.; Learning Curve:: You gain advantage on the next check with the same tool within 1 hour.

Name: Conversational Inertia
Rules Text: You understand how conversations accumulate momentum.
Rules Bullets: Momentum Gain:: After a successful Charisma check the next related Charisma check in the scene has advantage.; Soft Failure:: After a failed Charisma check hostile escalation requires an additional trigger.

Name: Improvised Standardization
Rules Text: You normalize chaos and substandard conditions.
Rules Bullets: Field Normalization:: Improvised or poor tool setups count as proper equipment.; Emergency Repair:: You can restore a broken tool to limited function without materials.

Name: Operational Planner
Rules Text: You think in supply chains and preparation.
Rules Bullets: Reduced Setup:: During downtime reduce group travel or setup time by 25 percent.; Supply Buffer:: Once per journey negate one resource depletion event.

Name: Parallel Focus
Rules Text: You divide attention between overlapping demands.
Rules Bullets: Split Focus:: You may concentrate on one spell and one non-spell effect simultaneously.; Unstable Focus:: Failing a concentration save ends only one effect chosen randomly.

Name: Preemptive Read
Rules Text: You read intent before action.
Rules Bullets: Early Read:: At the start of combat ask one yes-or-no question about enemy capabilities. Answer must be truthful but minimal.

Name: Maker’s Signature
Rules Text: Your work carries identity and consequence.
Rules Bullets: Recognizable Craft:: Items you create are identifiable as yours.; Reputation Weight:: NPC reactions shift based on familiarity with your work.

Name: Ordonnant Scarred
Rules Text: You were altered by contact with higher law.
Rules Bullets: Rule Enforcement:: Once per day declare an outcome invalid if it contradicts an established rule previously demonstrated in play.

Name: Environmental Calibration
Rules Text: You rapidly adapt to unfamiliar or hostile terrain.
Rules Bullets: Adaptive Posture:: After 10 minutes in a new environment you ignore one terrain-based penalty.; Situational Memory:: You have advantage on checks to navigate or operate in that environment for the next 24 hours.
Designer Notes: Terrain-first feat not tied to combat math

Name: Deferred Impact
Rules Text: You act with delayed intent.
Rules Bullets: Held Consequence:: Once per combat you may declare that a successful attack or effect resolves at the end of the target’s next turn instead of immediately.; Pressure Window:: Until it resolves the target has disadvantage on its next saving throw.

Name: Cognitive Offload
Rules Text: You externalize mental strain.
Rules Bullets: Stress Buffer:: When you fail a mental saving throw you may delay the effect until the end of your next turn.; Mental Triage:: If you use this feature you have disadvantage on Intelligence checks until your next short rest.

Name: Situational Authority
Rules Text: Your confidence fills uncertainty.
Rules Bullets: Command the Moment:: When no clear leader is present you gain advantage on one Charisma check to direct a group.; Stabilize Crowd:: Once per long rest you can prevent a neutral crowd from turning hostile.

Name: Material Intuition
Rules Text: You understand matter by touch and failure.
Rules Bullets: Read the Grain:: After handling an object you learn one property relevant to crafting or breaking it.; Waste Reduction:: Failed crafting attempts consume half the usual materials.

Name: Momentum Banking
Rules Text: You convert success into future advantage.
Rules Bullets: Stored Momentum:: When you succeed on a check by 5 or more you may store momentum.; Release Momentum:: Before your next long rest you may spend it to gain advantage on a related check.

Name: Boundary Sense
Rules Text: You feel the edges of reality.
Rules Bullets: Planar Tension:: You always know when you are within 60 feet of a planar boundary or weakened veil.; Stability Check:: You have advantage on saves against forced teleportation.

Name: Operational Redundancy
Rules Text: You plan for failure as a certainty.
Rules Bullets: Fallback Plan:: Once per long rest when a plan fails the group may immediately attempt an alternate approach without time loss.; Distributed Load:: Exhaustion from travel affects one fewer creature of your choice.

Name: Narrative Anchoring
Rules Text: You remember what should not change.
Rules Bullets: Anchor Event:: Once per arc you may declare one established fact immune to retcon or contradiction.; Continuity Sense:: You immediately notice when events diverge from established truth.

Name: Witness of Collapse
Rules Text: You have seen systems fail.
Rules Bullets: Predict Failure:: Once per day you may ask the DM what element of a situation is most likely to fail first.; Prepared Response:: Your first reaction to that failure has advantage.
